2605.06352.title: Topological Signatures of Grokking
2605.06352.paper_summary: We study the grokking phenomenon through the lens of topology. Using persistent homology on point clouds derived from the embedding matrices of a range of models trained on modular arithmetic with varying primes, we identify a clear and consistent topological signature of grokking: a sharp increase in both the maximum and total persistence of first homology ($H_1$). Persistence diagrams reveal the emergence of a dominant long-lived topological feature together with increasingly structured secondary features, reflecting the underlying cyclic structure of the task. Compared to existing spectral and geometric diagnostics -- specifically, Fourier analysis and local intrinsic dimension -- persistent homology provides a unified geometric and topological characterization of representation learning, capturing both local and global multi-scale structure. Ablations across data regimes and control settings show that these topological transitions are tied to generalization rather than memorization. Our results suggest that persistent homology offers a principled and interpretable framework for analyzing how neural networks internalize latent structure during training.
2605.06352.paper_summary_zh: 我們通過拓撲學的視角研究grokking現象。<lb/>利用持久同調對來自多種模型的嵌入矩陣衍生的點雲進行分析，這些模型是在不同質數的模算術上訓練的，我們識別出grokking的明確且一致的拓撲特徵：第一同調的最大持久性和總持久性均顯著增加（$H_1$）。<lb/>持久性圖揭示了一個主導的長壽命拓撲特徵的出現，以及越來越結構化的次要特徵，反映了任務的基礎循環結構。<lb/>與現有的光譜和幾何診斷相比——具體來說是傅里葉分析和局部內在維度——持久同調提供了一種統一的幾何和拓撲特徵來描述表示學習，捕捉到局部和全局的多尺度結構。<lb/>在數據範疇和控制設置中的消融實驗顯示，這些拓撲轉變與泛化相關，而非記憶。<lb/>我們的結果表明，持久同調為分析神經網絡在訓練過程中如何內化潛在結構提供了一個有原則且可解釋的框架。
2605.06352.author: Yifan Tang et.al.
2605.06352.authors: Yifan Tang, Qiquan Wang, Inés García-Redondo, Anthea Monod
